audit_status.source_base: Greek 1 John text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact draft reviewed for Word of life, seen/touched witness, fellowship, light, confession, cleansing.

verses.restored: What was from beginning, what we have heard, what we have seen with our eyes, what we looked upon and our hands touched, concerning the Word of life,
and the life was manifested, and we have seen and testify and announce to you the age-long life that was with the Father and was manifested to us,
what we have seen and heard we announce also to you, so that you also may have fellowship/participation with us; and indeed our fellowship is with the Father and with his Son Jesus Messiah.
And these things we write so that our joy may be made full.
And this is the message that we have heard from him and announce to you: that the Divine is light, and darkness is not in him at all.
If we say that we have fellowship with him and walk in darkness, we lie and do not do the truth.
But if we walk in the light as he is in the light, we have fellowship with one another, and the blood/life of Jesus his Son cleanses us from every sin/misalignment.
If we say that we have no sin/misalignment, we deceive ourselves, and the truth is not in us.
If we confess our sins/misalignments, he is trustworthy and righteous/just, so that he may release/forgive us the sins/misalignments and cleanse us from every injustice/unrighteousness.
If we say that we have not sinned/misaligned, we make him a liar, and his word is not in us.

translation_notes: 1 John begins with embodied witness: heard, seen, looked upon, touched.
Koinonia is fellowship/participation, not mere sentiment.
Walking in light includes truthful confession and cleansing, not denial of misalignment.
